title: LUTs not topologically sorted after write_verilog
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.9+2406 (git sha1 334ec5fa, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Given this verilog program (adder.v)

```
module adder(G11,G12,G14);
input [7:0] G11,G12;
output [7:0] G14;
assign G14 = G11+G12;
endmodule
```

Yosys script: `read_verilog adder.v; synth; abc -lut 6; write_verilog -noexpr -v adder_out.v` 

LUTs are not topologically sorted in adder_out.v

However, executing `read_verilog adder.v; synth; abc -g simple,-MUX; write_verilog -noexpr -v adder_out.v`  yields topologically sorted logic gates.

cc: @jimouris

### Expected Behavior

I would expect the LUTs to be topologically sorted in the generated Verilog.

### Actual Behavior

LUTs are not topologically sorted when dumped to a Verilog netlist.
